summary: The current data model stores only the latest order state in orders.status. To support status history, the codebase needs an append-only history table keyed by order_id plus application changes in the status transition path so each valid transition writes both the current status and a timestamped history record. The existing status column should stay in place because current reads and transition logic depend on it.

fileCitations.note: The orders table definition at lines 11-17 has a single text status column and no companion history table. This is the current source of truth for order state.
PATCH /:id/status at roughly lines 57-94 updates orders.status in place and does not persist prior states, which is the write path that would need to emit history records.

discoveredConstraints: Existing reads already expect orders.status to contain the current status, so removing or renaming it would break current behavior.
History should be append-only so operators can reconstruct the transition timeline.
Any schema change should preserve the existing transition rules in the admin-only status update route.
openQuestions: Should history also capture who performed the status change, or is timestamp plus status enough for the first iteration?
Should order history be returned from GET /api/orders/:id or exposed through a separate endpoint?
